extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won stage 4 via perfect Deceuninck-QuickStep leadout after Lotto-Soudal and Sunweb shredded sprinters' trains on Côte de Meron 15km out
tactical_patterns: Sunweb and Bora-Hansgrohe paced hard over Côte de Meron (15km out) specifically to isolate pure sprinters from their leadout trains
Deceuninck-QuickStep uniquely kept Cofidis-Reichenbach-Viviani intact through high-speed finale where rivals' trains fractured, revealing superior train discipline
Jumbo-Visma sprinters Groenewegen and Van Aert finishing 5th-6th separately suggests team confusion over sprint leadership allocation

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: first Tour stage win two months after disastrous Giro suggests late-season peaking pattern over grand tour duration
rider_signals.SUNWEB Team: neutral: pacing over final categorized climb fractured sprinters' trains but didn't deliver own sprint result, tactic benefits others
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: yellow jersey leading out at 1km indicates Quick-Step prioritizing his GC position security over pure sprint optimization
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Jumbo allowing both him and Van Aert to sprint independently reveals unclear team sprint hierarchy on contested stages
